name: git-advanced-workflows
description: Master advanced Git workflows including rebasing, cherry-picking, bisect, worktrees, and reflog to maintain clean history and recover from any situation. Use when managing complex Git histories, collaborating on feature branches, or troubleshooting repository issues.
capabilities: Explain interactive rebase operations and options
Provide cherry-pick commands for single commits or ranges
Describe manual and automated git bisect steps
Guide git worktree creation, listing, and cleanup
Outline reflog recovery for lost commits or branches
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing pure instructional content about Git workflows. No executable code, network calls, file access, or environment variable reads were found. All content is educational text explaining Git commands. Static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns, hash identifiers, and markdown code blocks.
valueStatement: Advanced Git history tasks can be risky and time consuming. This skill provides clear guidance for rebase, cherry-pick, bisect, worktrees, and reflog recovery.
userTitle: Master advanced Git workflows and recovery
limitations: Does not run Git commands or inspect repositories
Cannot resolve merge conflicts without user details
Does not validate project specific branching policies
No automation for cleanup scripts or hooks

useCases.title: Clean feature branch history
useCases.description: Prepare a linear commit history before opening a pull request.

useCases.title: Backport a hotfix safely
useCases.description: Apply one fix across multiple release branches with cherry-pick steps.

useCases.title: Locate a regression commit
useCases.description: Use bisect steps to find the commit that introduced a bug.

promptTemplates.title: Rebase basics
promptTemplates.scenario: Clean last five commits before PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Show the exact interactive rebase steps to squash and reword my last five commits safely.

promptTemplates.title: Cherry-pick a fix
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to cherry-pick a specific commit onto release/2.0 and how to continue after conflicts.

promptTemplates.title: Bisect with tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Give a step by step git bisect workflow using a test command to locate a regression.

promptTemplates.title: Worktrees and recovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Describe how to use worktrees for parallel branches and how to recover a lost commit with reflog.

bestPractices: Create a backup branch before risky history edits
Keep commits small and descriptive for easier recovery
antiPatterns: Rebasing public branches used by collaborators
Force pushing without checking remote updates
Running bisect with a dirty working directory

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Git versions?
faq.answer: It works with modern Git versions that support rebase, bisect, worktree, and reflog.

faq.question: Does it change my repository automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance only and does not run commands or modify files.

faq.question: Can it integrate with my CI or scripts?
faq.answer: It can suggest steps, but it does not execute or configure CI pipelines.

faq.question: Does it access my data or credentials?
faq.answer: No. It does not read files, environment variables, or network resources.

faq.question: What if I cannot resolve a conflict?
faq.answer: Share the conflict details and current status to get targeted steps.

faq.question: How is this different from basic Git help?
faq.answer: It focuses on advanced workflows like bisect, reflog recovery, and worktrees.
